text: You're about to prepare this procedure using the Incision Academy accredited by the Royal College of Surgeons. For your orientation the positioning of the body is shown. Here you can see important landmarks which will guide the subsequent incision. Our procedures are structured with a clear step-by-step approach. Here you will see the steps of the procedure.
The surgeon observes a hydropic gallbladder and pus within the peritoneal space, indicating inflammation and infection.

Under camera guidance, the surgeon inserts a suprapubic trocar and a third trocar in the left lower abdominal space. The peritoneal space is inspected, revealing the appendix typically located in the right lower quadrant, along with the terminal ileum and uterus. Laparoscopic instruments are used to dissect adhesions from the abdominal wall.

The surgeon grasps the base of the appendix with a clamp, visualizing the infected appendix and mesoappendix, confirming perforated appendicitis. The base of the appendix is ligated with a laparoscopic loop, and the appendix is cut between the clamp and the loop, with the cecal pool visible in the surgical field.

To get a quicker and more thorough preparation for the OR, click on the link below. There you will find an anatomy section relevant to this chapter, as well as a test to see if you're ready. Good luck with the preparation.
